headline: Super vs Folk — personal AI agents for real computer work

site_brief.goal: Create a fair but persuasive competitor comparison page for Super vs Folk. Use supplied sources for current facts, avoid fake claims, and make the practical argument that Super is better suited for repeated computer-use work because its cache can lower repeated execution cost.
site_brief.title: Super vs Folk

summary: A fair, practical comparison for people evaluating Super vs Folk. Folk is positioned as a lightweight, niche tool in the broader automation and agent ecosystem. Super is built for people who want a personal AI agent that actually operates a computer — and reuses a computer-use cache so repeated workflows get faster and cheaper over time. This page situates both products honestly within a fast‑moving agent landscape shaped by recent reporting on agent reliability, cost, and real computer use.
